The '''Adventurer''' was a [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]] design, roughly the size of a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]].

==Characteristics==
Designed as a personal sporting craft before the ascent of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Adventurer looked sleek and speedy, but this outward appearance was misleading - its actual performance capabilities were decidedly modest, and its crew compartment not particularly comfortable.<ref name="BtS">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref>

The hull was an aerodynamic [[Wikipedia:lifting body|lifting body]] design, providing additional lift in atmospheric flight, where it used [[hoverjet]]s in place of [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] for propulsion.<ref name="BtS" /> The realspace thrusters for space travel had a modest class 2.5 rating.<ref name="BtS" /> To reduce the construction cost of the ship, the Adventurer's main [[power bus]] had a relatively modest capability, incapable of simultaneously handling the realspace propulsion and navigation systems and those associated with the hyperdrive - instead, it was fitted with a [[cascade processor]] which managed their sequential deactivation and activation during transit to and from lightspeed, but which was vulnerable to power surges during reversion to realspace.<ref name="TT">''[[Tyrant's Test]]''</ref>

Although there was a competent [[Hypercomm/Legends|hypercomm]],<ref name="SL">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref> as well as a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navcom]] and and a [[Navigation deflector system|navigational deflector]],<ref name="BtS" /> the type was not equipped with anything resembling military-grade [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] as standard, nor with weapons or an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[Astromech socket/Legends|slot]].<ref name="BtS" /> In addition, the hyperdrive was configured with a FCZ lockout, designed to prevent it from making the jump to lightspeed inside a [[Flight Control Zone]] such as planetary orbit. The ship's computer interface did not allow systems such as the FCZ lockout to be modified or overridden.<ref name="BtS" />

Nonetheless, upgrades to the systems were available - for example, the navigational deflector could be improved beyond the basic Block 1 configuration to at least a Block 3 standard.<ref name="BtS" /> Hotwiring some components was also relatively simple, such as deactivating the FCZ lockout, which was connected to the [[nav controller]] rather than the [[Hyperdrive motivator/Legends|hyperdrive motivator]].<ref name="SL" />

The boarding hatch was accessed by a somewhat slender-looking boarding ladder.<ref name="SL" /> Internally, the Adventurer had a single open cabin, which combined a relatively spacious flight deck area with a forward-facing viewport and seats for a pilot and co-pilot, and an accommodation section with a single bunk and a cramped [[refresher]], both of them screened by curtains.<ref name="BtS" /> There was also a cargo hold, which could accommodate a reasonable quantity of supplies and luggage,<ref name="BtS" /> and a floor hatch in the deck gave internal maintenance access to the drive compartment.<ref name="SL" /> At least some ships also had a [[meal service console]] which could serve three different drinks and a variety of food options.<ref name="BtS" />

At least in the era of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the vintage ship was considered unsuitable for any activity beyond pleasure rides, or at best, short business trips to outlying systems that were not served by regular commercial flights.<ref name="BtS" />

==History==
One particular Adventurer was used by [[Akanah Norand Goss Pell]], who travelled aboard it with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]]. Luke gave the ship the name ''[[Mud Sloth]]'', reflecting its dawdling performance compared with the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] he was used to.
